title: Estimate microstrip width for a target impedance
description: Use the stackup dielectric height and constant to estimate the trace width that yields the target single-ended impedance.
body: Use when you need a starting trace width for a controlled-impedance microstrip. Do not finalize width without the fab's confirmed dielectric, since the estimate shifts with the real stackup. Actions: take the dielectric height and constant from the stackup, estimate the width for the target impedance, and verify against a field-solver or fab report. Validation signals include an estimated width whose calculated impedance lands within tolerance and agreement with the fab report. Risk boundary: an estimate based on a guessed stackup can be off by a fab tolerance, so flag the width as provisional until the dielectric is confirmed.
